m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output?
let nums = [1, 2, 3, 4];
let sliced = nums.slice(1, 3);
console.log(sliced);
m_Item1.Explanation: slice(1, 3) returns elements from index 1 to 2 (not including 3).

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to append a value to an array of Java Script?
m_Item1.Explanation: To append values to an array use arr[arr.length] = value it will add at the end of array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of these is a correct method to create a new array?
m_Item1.Explanation: To create a new Array use :  var myArray = [];

m_Item1.QuestionText:
var data = [“A”, “B”, “C”, “D”];
data.unshift(“X”);
data.push(“Y”);
What does data look like?
m_Item1.Explanation: unshift() method adds element at the begining of array and push() method will add element at the end of array.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider this code:
var x = ['Hello'];
What value will 'x[1]' return?
m_Item1.Explanation: x[0]  gives 'Hello'  only one element in array at index 0.So x[1] returns  undefined.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is the correct way to write a JavaScript array?
m_Item1.Explanation: var txt = new Array('arr ','kim','jim')

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to append a value to an array of Java Script?
m_Item1.Explanation: To append a value to an array use arr[arr.length] = value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you remove an element from an array in javascript and replace it with a new one ?
m_Item1.Explanation: array.splice(start index,count of element,new element to replace)  method add/remove an element from existing array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following code creates an object?
m_Item1.Explanation: In JavaScript object can be created by using Object's constructor, Object's create method and bracket syntax.

m_Item1.QuestionText: . Which built-in method calls a function for each element in the array?
m_Item1.Explanation: forEach method calls a function passed as an argument for every element in the array

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which built-in method adds one or more elements to the end of an array and returns the new length of the array?
m_Item1.Explanation: push() method allows to add the element at the end of the array

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object returns true if every element in this array satisfies the provided testing function?
m_Item1.Explanation: every() function checks the condition given is true for each element in the array and then only returns true

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object adds one or more elements to the front of an array and returns the new length of the array?
m_Item1.Explanation: unshift() function is used to add the element at the beginning of array

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the observation made in the following JavaScript code?
var count = [1,,3];

Options.OptionText: The omitted value takes an integer value

m_Item1.Explanation: Value at index 1 is omitted which will start the value as undefined by default.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will be the possible output of the following JavaScript code?
var a = [1,2,3,4,5];
a.slice(0,3);

What will be the output of the following JavaScript code?
var sum=0;
var arr = [10,15,20,30];  
 rr.forEach(function myFunction(element)
{  
        sum= sum+element;  
});  
document.writeln(sum);
m_Item1.Explanation: forEach is a predefined function in Javascript which is used to traverse through the array. It works in a similar way like for loop and iterates through each value in array.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will be the firstname and surname of the following JavaScript code?

var book = {
              "main title": "JavaScript",
              'sub-title': "The Definitive Guide",
              "for": "all audiences",
              author: {
                         firstname: "David",
                         surname: "Flanagan"
                      }
           };
m_Item1.Explanation:
The above code snippet contains an object inside another object. firstname and surname are the property names.
The value of that particular property is itself an object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a valid type of function java script supports?
m_Item1.Explanation: Javascript allows us to use names as well as anonymous functions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Can you assign a anonymous function to a variable?
m_Item1.Explanation: Anonymous functions can be used to assign to a variable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Can you pass a anonymous function as an argument to another function?
m_Item1.Explanation: Anonymous functions can be used as an argument to another function which is called in a callback fashion.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object is similar to iterating the elements in the array?
m_Item1.Explanation: forEach() function is used to iterate array.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object creates a new array with all of the elements of this array for
which the provided filtering function returns true?
m_Item1.Explanation: array.filter() method returns a new array consisting of only those elements that satisfied condition.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object returns the first least index of an element within the array
equal to the specified value, or -1 if none is found?
m_Item1.Explanation: indexOf(element,index) method returns first least index of an element with array equal to specified value.if not found then returns -1.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object joins all elements of an array into a string?
m_Item1.Explanation: join() method will join all the elements of an array to a string.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object returns the last greatest index of an element within the
array equal to the specified value, or -1 if none is found?
m_Item1.Explanation: lastIndexOf() method returns last greatest index of an element within the array equal to specified value. If not found the  returns -1.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object creates a new array with the results of calling a provided
function on every element in this array?
m_Item1.Explanation: map() function creates a new array with the results of calling a specified function on every element in this array.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object removes the last element from an array and returns that
element?
m_Item1.Explanation: pop() method removes last element from an array and return it.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object applies a function simultaneously against two values of the
array fromleft - to - right as to reduce it to a single value?
m_Item1.Explanation: reduce()

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object applies a function simultaneously against two values of the
array from right - to - left as to reduce it to a single value?
m_Item1.Explanation: rediceRight()

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object reverses the order of the elements of an array?
m_Item1.Explanation: reverse()

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object removes the first element from an array and returns that
element?
m_Item1.Explanation: shift() removes first element and returns it.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object extracts a section of an array and returns a new array?
m_Item1.Explanation: slice() extracts a section of an array and return new array.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object returns true if at least one element in this array satisfies the
provided testing function?
m_Item1.Explanation: some() method returns true of false.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object represents the source code of an object?
m_Item1.Explanation: toSource() function of array object represents the source code of an object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object sorts the elements of an array?
m_Item1.Explanation: sort() method sorts the element of array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following function of Array object returns a string representing the array and its elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: toString() function returns string representation of array elements..

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object adds one or more elements to the front of an array and
returns the new length of the array?
m_Item1.Explanation: unshift() function adds one or more elements to the front of an array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which built-in method reverses the order of the elements of an array?
m_Item1.Explanation: reverse() method will reverse the order of the elements of an array.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
<script>
var numbers = [45,4,9,16,25];
var flag = numbers.some(myFun);
document.write(flag)
function myFun(value,index,array)
{
    return value > 10
}
</script>
m_Item1.Explanation: some() function checks whether some of the array elements pass the test

m_Item1.QuestionText:
var arr = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "date"];
arr.splice(1, 2);
console.log(arr);
What will be the output of the console.log(arr) statement?
m_Item1.Explanation: The splice() function is used to change the contents of an array by removing or replacing existing elements and/or adding new elements. In this example, splice(1, 2) removes two elements starting from index 1 (inclusive). So, it removes "banana" and "cherry", leaving ["apple", "date"] in the array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method creates a new array with the results of calling a provided function on every element in an array?
m_Item1.Explanation: The map() method creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will the following code output? console.log([1, 2, 3, 4].pop());
m_Item1.Explanation: The pop() method removes the last element from an array and returns that element. Here, '4' is removed and returned.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods would you use to add an element at the start of an array?
m_Item1.Explanation: The unshift() method adds new items to the beginning of an array and returns the new length.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the splice() method do in arrays?

Options.OptionText: It creates a shallow copy of a portion of an array.
It adds/removes items to/from an array and returns the removed items.
It reverses the order of elements in an array.
It sorts the elements of an array.

m_Item1.Explanation: The splice() method changes the contents of an array by removing or replacing existing elements and/or adding new elements in place.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which array method will change an array into a string that is separated by specified characters?
m_Item1.Explanation: The join() method joins all elements of an array into a string and separates them with a specified delimiter.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you check if a variable is an array in JavaScript?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Array.isArray() method returns true if an object is an array, false if it is not.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method removes the first element from an array and returns that element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The shift() method removes the first element from an array and returns that removed element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary difference between the slice() and splice() methods?

Options.OptionText: slice() modifies the array, splice() does not.
splice() modifies the array, slice() does not.
slice() adds elements, splice() only removes elements.
splice() adds elements, slice() only removes elements.

m_Item1.Explanation: The slice() method returns a shallow copy of a portion of an array without modifying the original array, while splice() modifies the array in place.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you reverse the order of elements in an array?
m_Item1.Explanation: The reverse() method reverses the order of the elements in an array in place.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will the following code output? console.log([].concat([1,2], [3,4]));
m_Item1.Explanation: The concat() method is used to merge two or more arrays into a new array without changing the existing arrays.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which JavaScript array method creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function?
m_Item1.Explanation: The filter() method creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which array method in JavaScript is used to add one or more elements to the end of an array, returning the new length of the array?
m_Item1.Explanation: The push() method adds one or more elements to the end of an array and returns the new length of the array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the map() method do in JavaScript?

Options.OptionText: Removes the last element from an array
Executes a provided function once for each array element
Creates a new array with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array
Flattens a nested array to a specified depth

m_Item1.Explanation: The map() method creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does the splice() method affect an array?

Options.OptionText: Returns a shallow copy of a portion of an array
Adds/removes items to/from an array, modifying the original array
Reverses the order of the elements in an array
Sorts the elements of an array

m_Item1.Explanation: The splice() method adds or removes items to/from an array, which modifies the contents of the original array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method in JavaScript is used to join all elements of an array into a string?
m_Item1.Explanation: The join() method joins all elements of an array into a string, with the option to specify a separator.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the reduce() method return after executing?
m_Item1.Explanation: The reduce() method executes a reducer function on each element of the array, resulting in a single value return.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method can be used to find the first element in an array that satisfies a testing function?
m_Item1.Explanation: The find() method returns the value of the first element in the array that satisfies the provided testing function.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If you need to add elements to an array at the beginning, which method should you use?
m_Item1.Explanation: The unshift() method adds one or more elements to the beginning of an array and returns the new length of the array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which array method would you use to remove the first element from an array and return that element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The shift() method removes the first element from an array and returns that element. This method changes the length of the array.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which JavaScript array method would you use to execute a provided function once for each array element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The forEach() method executes a provided function once for each array element.
